title: Drive My Car
overview: A stage actor and director grieving the loss of his wife finds unexpected solace in his relationship with his young female chauffeur as they journey together to Hiroshima for a production of Uncle Vanya.

title: Shoplifters
overview: A couple who live with their respective families in a cramped house discover a little girl left out in the cold. After bringing her home, they must come to grips with the true meaning of family.

title: Departures
overview: A young cellist loses his job and returns to his hometown in rural Japan, where he discovers a profound and unexpected career preparing the deceased for burial in the ancient nokanshi tradition.

title: Like Father, Like Son
overview: A successful businessman discovers that his 6-year-old son was switched at birth with another boy from a less affluent family, forcing both families to grapple with what it truly means to be a father.

title: Our Little Sister
overview: Three sisters invite their teenage half-sister to come live with them in their ancestral home in Kamakura after their father's death, discovering warmth and family bonds they never expected.

title: After the Storm
overview: A struggling novelist and part-time private detective must reconcile his past failures when a typhoon traps him overnight with his estranged ex-wife, young son, and elderly mother.

title: Tampopo
overview: A truck driver stops at a small noodle shop run by a young widow and resolves to help her perfect her ramen recipe, in this irreverent and joyful comedy that weaves together food, desire, and the art of eating.

title: Sweet Bean
overview: A middle-aged man who runs a small pancake stand is transformed when an elderly woman offers to make the sweet red bean filling for him, bringing unexpected warmth to both their lives.

title: The Great Passage
overview: A socially awkward young editor joins a publishing house's long-running project to compile a new Japanese dictionary, discovering that choosing exactly the right words is a profound act of love.

title: Nobody Knows
overview: Four children, fathered by different men, are left alone in a Tokyo apartment by their free-spirited mother. They must quietly survive the year without attracting the attention of their neighbors.

title: Tokyo Story
overview: An elderly couple travel to Tokyo to visit their grown children, only to find them too busy to spend much time with them, in Yasujiro Ozu's masterpiece about the distance between generations.

title: Early Summer
overview: A young woman working in Tokyo resists her family's pressure to marry a suitable match, navigating duty, desire, and the changing nature of the post-war Japanese family.

title: The Taste of Tea
overview: A quirky family living in rural Tochigi Prefecture navigates love, career ambitions, and a young girl haunted by a giant apparition of herself, in a gently surreal portrait of everyday life.

title: Midnight Diner
overview: The Master opens his small Tokyo diner only between midnight and seven in the morning. Through the simple meals he cooks and the people who come seeking them, life's small dramas unfold.

title: Kitchen
overview: A young woman orphaned after her grandmother's death finds unexpected solace living with a young man and his transgender mother, exploring how food, warmth, and chosen family can heal grief.

title: Still Walking
overview: Over the course of a single summer day, a family gathers at their parents' home for the annual memorial of a son who drowned years ago, revealing the tensions and tenderness held quietly between them.

title: Broker
overview: Two men who run an illegal baby-broker service team up with a young mother and her baby, forming an unlikely family as they travel across South Korea in search of the perfect adoptive parents.

title: An
overview: An elderly woman applies for a job at a taiyaki stall selling small dorayaki pancakes. As her extraordinary sweet bean paste recipe revives the shop, the past she carries slowly comes to light.

title: Julie & Julia
overview: Julia Child's story of her start in the cooking profession is intertwined with blogger Julie Powell's 2002 challenge to cook all 524 recipes in Child's landmark cookbook in 365 days.

title: Jiro Dreams of Sushi
overview: An 85-year-old sushi master runs a tiny restaurant in a Tokyo subway station that has earned three Michelin stars. A documentary portrait of obsession, craft, and the pursuit of perfection.

title: Okja
overview: A young girl risks everything to prevent a powerful multinational company from taking her best friend, a giant animal named Okja, to New York as part of a corporate branding scheme.

title: My Neighbor Totoro
overview: Two young sisters move to the country with their father and discover that the surrounding forest is home to magical creatures, including the large and lovable forest spirit Totoro.
